title: The Music of Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan Pulled Me Through The Grief of Family Loss
description:
The following things happen in my body when I think about writing this story: my chest tightens, my breathing gets quicker, shallow; a tingling sensation covers my arms; the skin on my forehead seems to tighten itself around my brain. I mostly think of the story in the shower where nothing but a stream of […]

The post The Music of Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan Pulled Me
hook: Sensory, grief-stricken essay on how Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan's music became a vessel for family loss and bodily memory.

title: The Film Comment Podcast: Akinola Davies Jr. on My Father’s Shadow
description: Family portrait: the director talks about his elliptical, atmospheric debut feature set in 1993 Nigeria
hook: Director discusses his elliptical, atmospheric debut set in 1993 Nigeria—family portraiture as cinematic form.

title: Day Care
description:
‘Day care meant sick baby. And sick baby meant she had to skip work to care for sick baby, and entertain sick baby with bad mom art.’

A short story by Nora Lange.

The post Day Care appeared first on Granta.
hook: Granta short story exploring a mother's interior life through the mundane crisis of day care and creative exhaustion.

title: This novel about family drama is so good you may want to re-read it immediately
description: Allegra Goodman's new novel is called This Is Not About Us, but critic Maureen Corrigan says that title is coy: Readers are bound to see aspects of themselves and their families in these pages.
hook: Critic Maureen Corrigan reveals why Allegra Goodman's family drama demands rereading—psychology embedded in plot.

title: Scholar, seductress, alchemist: who was the real Cleopatra?
description:
The Egyptian queen has fascinated me from childhood, but following the archives led only to ancient gossip and Roman propaganda. Fiction was the way to liberate her from misogynist myth

Witch, whore, villain – there are few women who have been as vilified through history as Cleopatra VII. The disdain of ancient sources that sought to dismiss her as exotic and seductive has corrupted her legacy. But I take pleasure in knowing that her name has permeated through time with far more recogni
hook: Scholar reclaims Cleopatra from misogynist myth through archive, propaganda, and the liberatory power of fiction.

title: Interview: Werner Herzog on ‘Ghost Elephants’ and Hunting a Dream in the Angolan Highlands
description:
Herzog discusses the genesis of his new documentary and the urgency of his filmmaking.

The post Int
hook: Werner Herzog on obsession, dream-hunting, and cinema as tool for chasing ghosts in the Angolan highlands.

title: The Jazz Pictures the FBI Silenced
description: Fearing for her safety, Lisette Model buried her photos of artists like Billie Holiday and Louis Armstrong, but a new book reveals them to the world.
hook: Lisette Model's buried photographs of Billie Holiday and Louis Armstrong reveal jazz history the FBI tried to silence.
